name: Potato Cakes (Bakestone Recipes)
recipeInstructions: Potato cakes are traditionally served with bacon and sausages. For best results, use hot, freshly cooked potatoes. If using cold potatoes, melt the butter before adding it. Choose a floury type of potato and boil in well-salted water until tender. Drain the potatoes thoroughly, then return them to low heat in the same pan. Cover the pan with a dishcloth and allow the potatoes to dry for 5-10 minutes (known as "drying in their steam" in Ireland). They should be dry and floury at the end of this process. Add salt and sift into a mixing bowl. Beat in the softened butter until incorporated. Gradually add enough self-raising flour to form a soft dough that is easy to handle. Turn out onto a floured surface and roll or pat into rounds about ¾ inch thick. Cut with a 3-inch scone cutter. Place on a hot, greased bakestone and cook over moderate heat until golden brown on the underside. Flip and cook the other side. Remove from the bakestone, split each cake, spread generously with butter, and close again. Keep warm while preparing additional batches. Serve hot.
